title: Agentic Enterprise Architecture
subtitle: EA as an Agentic Function
description: Moving from describing how EA must change to building EA that operates through AI agents on graph-based repositories.

hero.headline: From Framework to Function
hero.subline: Enterprise Architecture becomes an agentic capability — AI agents that operate on living architecture models to produce deliverables, assess impact, and guide decisions in real time.

sections.title: The Vision: EA as an Agentic Function
sections.content: Traditional EA produces documents and diagrams that become stale the moment they are published. Agentic EA inverts this: <mark>the repository is the living architecture</mark>, and AI agents continuously operate on it to produce deliverables on demand. The architect's role shifts from document author to repository curator and agent supervisor.

points.title: Living Repository
points.description: Architecture models maintained in a graph database, auto-collected from source systems, always current. No more PowerPoint archaeology.

points.title: Agent Personas
points.description: Specialized AI agents (Business Architect, Governance Agent, Repository Agent) with defined personas, tools, and output templates.

points.title: On-Demand Deliverables
points.description: Gap analyses, impact assessments, ADRs, and stakeholder views generated in minutes from the live repository — not weeks from interviews.

points.title: Human-in-the-Loop
points.description: Architects review, validate, and refine agent outputs. Agents handle data gathering and pattern recognition; humans provide judgment and context.

sections.title: Agent-Repository Architecture
sections.content: The architecture follows a clean three-tier pattern: <mark>Graph Repository → MCP Tool Layer → Agent Personas</mark>. Each tier has a distinct responsibility.

tiers.name: Graph Repository (Neo4j)
tiers.details: ArchiMate 3.2 metamodel with AI extensions. Native relationship types enable multi-hop traversal. Full-text search, constraint validation, viewpoint enforcement.

tiers.name: MCP Tool Layer (32 tools)
tiers.details: Python MCP server exposing query, mutation, analysis, viewpoint, and validation tools. Tools provide structured data; they don't interpret or advise.

tiers.name: Agent Personas
tiers.details: Claude Code with specialized CLAUDE.md personas. Each agent has defined use cases, output templates, and domain expertise. The persona layer is where business judgment happens.

sections.title: ArchiMate Extensions for AI
sections.content: Standard ArchiMate lacks concepts for AI agents, models, and knowledge bases. Using the ArchiMate Profile/Stereotype mechanism, we extend the metamodel without breaking compliance. <mark>Stereotypes inherit all valid relationships from their parent type</mark>.

stereotypes.name: AI Agent
stereotypes.description: Autonomous agent that perceives, reasons, and acts within the enterprise

stereotypes.name: AI Model
stereotypes.description: LLM, ML model, or other AI model deployed in the enterprise

stereotypes.name: Knowledge Base
stereotypes.description: Structured knowledge store for RAG, reasoning, or agent context

stereotypes.name: Vector Store
stereotypes.description: Embedding database for semantic search and retrieval

stereotypes.name: Agent Protocol
stereotypes.description: MCP, A2A, or custom agent communication endpoint

stereotypes.name: Prompt Template
stereotypes.description: Reusable prompt definition for agent behavior

stereotypes.name: Guardrail
stereotypes.description: Safety or quality gate validating agent inputs/outputs

sections.title: Use Cases
sections.content: Five use cases demonstrate the full range of agentic EA deliverables, from strategic analysis to operational views.

cases.title: Capability Gap Analysis
cases.input: Strategic objective (e.g., 'Implement predictive maintenance using AI agents')
cases.output: Structured gap analysis with coverage classification, supporting applications, and prioritized recommendations

cases.title: Impact Assessment
cases.input: Proposed change (e.g., 'Replace legacy SCADA with cloud-native platform')
cases.output: Multi-layer impact analysis with severity classification, safety-critical flags, and business process traces

cases.title: Process Agent-Readiness
cases.output: Per-sub-process readiness scores (1-5), human-in-the-loop requirements, and redesign recommendations

cases.title: Architecture Decision Record
cases.output: Standard ADR with 3+ options, principle alignment, consequences, and element-level impact

cases.title: Stakeholder View
cases.input: Viewpoint request (e.g., 'Grid Automation technology landscape for CTO')
cases.output: ArchiMate-compliant view with stakeholder-appropriate narrative and exportable JSON

sections.title: Graph-Based EA Repository
sections.content: The shift from document-based to <mark>graph-based EA repositories</mark> is foundational. A property graph naturally represents ArchiMate's element-relationship model, and native traversal enables the multi-hop queries that make impact analysis and dependency tracing possible.

comparison.graph: Property graph with typed nodes and relationships
